canonical_definition: The property that each predicted token depends on only a small, fixed number of previous tokens, which enables length generalization when this number does not grow with sequence length.
papers: SAFE: Finding Sparse and Flat Minima to Improve Pruning
The Role of Sparsity for Length Generalization in LLMs

relationships.paper: The Role of Sparsity for Length Generalization in LLMs
relationships.evidence: our experiments adjust the sparsity of several synthetic tasks, and we observe that length generalization improves monotonically with decreasing sparsity. (Section 1)
